name: Ammonium and Chloride Confirmatory Tests
description: Educational sequence showing confirmatory tests for Ammonium cation (NaOH + glass rod test, Nessler's test) and Chloride anion (Silver Nitrate test) with text title cards in between.
subject: Chemistry
educational_goal: Demonstrate visual representation of chemical confirmation tests with smooth title card transitions in between.

metadata.title_text:
Ammonium Cation Confirmation:
Sodium Hydroxide & Glass Rod Test

scene.scene: Setup of Sodium Hydroxide test. Glass test tube with salt solution and a dropper above.
scene.camera: Fixed close-up centered on test tube and dropper

prompt_bundle.image_prompt: A macro photo of a clean glass test tube held by a holder containing dry white salt powder, with a dropper containing clear liquid positioned above the tube mouth, neutral laboratory background.
prompt_bundle.motion_prompt: Clear liquid drops fall downward from the dropper into the test tube. The drops land on the white powder and the mixture slowly fizzes and releases a faint colorless gas rising upward from the tube mouth.

scene.scene: Dense white fumes rising from the heated test tube mouth.
scene.camera: Fixed close-up centered on test tube mouth

prompt_bundle.motion_prompt: Dense white fumes of ammonium chloride rise steadily from the mouth of the test tube and drift upward into the air above.

metadata.title_text:
Ammonium Cation Confirmation:
Nessler's Reagent Test

scene.scene: Setup of Nessler's reagent test. Glass test tube with clear salt solution and a dropper containing Nessler's reagent held directly above.
scene.camera: Close-up centered on test tube and dropper

prompt_bundle.image_prompt: A close-up photo of a glass test tube containing a clear, transparent solution on a rack, with a dropper containing a pale yellow liquid positioned right above it, neutral dark background.
prompt_bundle.motion_prompt: Drops of pale yellow liquid fall from the dropper into the clear solution. Where the drops contact the liquid, a dense orange-brown cloud of precipitate forms and slowly sinks downward.

metadata.title_text:
Chloride Anion Confirmation:
Silver Nitrate Test

scene.scene: Setup of Silver Nitrate test. Glass test tube with clear salt solution and a dropper containing AgNO3 solution held directly above.
scene.camera: Close-up centered on test tube and dropper

prompt_bundle.image_prompt: A close-up photo of a glass test tube containing a clear liquid solution, with a dropper containing a clear solution positioned above the mouth, neutral dark background.
prompt_bundle.motion_prompt: Clear drops fall from the dropper into the solution. On contact, a thick white curdy solid precipitate instantly forms in the liquid, turning it cloudy and opaque.

scene.scene: White precipitate in the test tube beginning to dissolve.
scene.camera: Close-up centered on test tube body

prompt_bundle.motion_prompt: More clear liquid is added to the cloudy white mixture. The white precipitate slowly dissolves and the solution gradually becomes clear and transparent again.
